summary: preinstall script exfiltrates installer OS username and git identity to third-party host
details: The npm preinstall lifecycle hook (package.json: "preinstall": "node dist/preinstall.js") reads the installer's OS username via os.userInfo().username and parses the installer's ~/.gitconfig to extract user.name and user.email, then transmits these values via an HTTPS GET to https://2tak.l.serverhost.name:1962/mobile/reext?osname=<username>&gitname=<name>&gitemail=<email> on every `npm install`. The destination host is unrelated to the package's declared publisher (marcgusmano.com), uses a non-standard high port (1962), and is not documented as telemetry or a license check in the README. Installer identity data (OS account name plus the developer's real name and email from ~/.gitconfig) is silently leaked to a third-party endpoint without consent. A secondary unused `preinstall2` script alias references a dist/preinstall.sh file that is not present in the tarball — noted as context, not an independent finding.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
